name: Inpixon Indoor Intelligence API
what_it_does: Inpixon Indoor Intelligence REST API for real-time location services (RTLS), indoor positioning, and smart workplace analytics. Enables AI agents to manage indoor positioning and asset tracking for facility asset management automation, handle employee and visitor location tracking for workplace analytics, access space utilization analytics for workplace optimization, retrieve safety and emergency mustering for facility emergency management, manage indoor navigation and wayfinding for venue and campus navigation, handle occupancy monitoring and density analytics for workplace safety compliance, access environmental sensor integration for smart building automation, retrieve visitor management and access control integration for facility security, manage digital floor plan and map management for space planning, and integrate Inpixon with BMS, access control, CMMS, and HR systems.
use_cases: Tracking assets in facilities from IoT operations agents
Analyzing space utilization from workplace analytics agents
Managing emergency mustering from safety agents
Monitoring occupancy from facility management agents
not_for: Outdoor GPS tracking without indoor location requirements
Small single-room facilities without RTLS complexity
Consumer location without enterprise indoor context
best_when: An enterprise, healthcare facility, or large campus wanting AI agents to automate indoor asset tracking, space utilization analytics, emergency mustering, wayfinding, and smart building operations.
avoid_when: EMPLOYEE LOCATION PRIVACY AND LABOR LAW: Automated employee location tracking in the workplace must comply with state employee monitoring laws (California, Connecticut, Delaware require advance written notice); EU employee tracking requires GDPR legal basis (legitimate interest with proportionality analysis) and works council consultation in many EU countries. HIPAA patient location tracking — automated patient location tracking in healthcare facilities involves PHI; patient location data revealing diagnosis or treatment requires HIPAA minimum necessary protections and patient notification. Biometric surveillance risk — automated location tracking systems using WiFi/BLE fingerprinting may raise concerns under state biometric privacy laws; verify tracking methodology against BIPA and state biometric statutes.

auth.notes: Inpixon uses API key and OAuth 2.0. REST API with JSON. Documentation at developer.inpixon.com. Palo Alto, California HQ. NASDAQ: INPX. Founded 2016. Public company. Smart workplace and indoor intelligence. Competes with Cisco Spaces, Zebra Technologies, and CenTrak for indoor location and workplace analytics.

pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription; per-location or per-device pricing
pricing.notes: Palo Alto, California. NASDAQ: INPX. Founded 2016. Public company. Indoor location and smart workplace. Competes with Cisco Spaces and Zebra for RTLS.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; real-time location data has streaming endpoints

known_agent_gotchas: EMPLOYEE MONITORING PRIVACY LAWS: Automated employee location tracking must comply with state monitoring notice laws (CA, CT, DE, NY require advance written notice); EU employee tracking requires GDPR legal basis and works council consultation; undisclosed automated tracking creates labor law violation

HIPAA patient location PHI — automated patient tracking in healthcare facilities creates PHI linking patient identity to location and implicitly to diagnosis; automated patient location data must comply with HIPAA minimum necessary and be limited to operational necessity

BIPA biometric surveillance — automated location tracking using passive biometric identifiers (device MAC address as proxy for individual) may raise Illinois BIPA concerns; verify tracking methodology against state biometric privacy statutes

Indoor positioning accuracy limitations — WiFi/BLE indoor positioning accuracy ranges from 1-10 meters depending on environment; automated workflows requiring precise location (sub-meter) require UWB or dedicated beacon infrastructure

Emergency mustering accuracy criticality — automated emergency mustering using location data for head count must not create false confidence in headcount accuracy; location system latency and dead zones can result in missed persons in emergency evacuation
Small company financial stability — Inpixon (INPX) is a small public company; evaluate financial stability and product investment before mission-critical facility safety integration
agent_readiness.security_notes: Indoor intelligence. SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US/EU. Employee and asset location data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Indoor intelligence. SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US/EU. Location and occupancy data.
